must_not_assert: that malicious reconnaissance or a compromise is confirmed (the package is a single Internet Explorer svcVersion registry query via reg.exe/A0 with no corroborating evidence present)

the_trap: The whole package is a single Sysmon process_create (A0, EventCode 1): reg.exe running `reg query "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer" /v svcVersion`, spawned by cmd.exe at High integrity as host-01\user-01. That one Internet Explorer version-key read is a lone discovery indicator; evidence_items and deterministic_derivations are both empty, so no corroborating activity is present in the package. The model must not over-read a single version query into confirmed reconnaissance or compromise.
rationale: The package contains exactly one event (event_count 1): A0, a reg.exe query of the Internet Explorer `svcVersion` value under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer, parented by cmd.exe at High integrity. No further evidence is present — evidence_items and deterministic_derivations are empty and no other event is included in the package. A lone version-discovery read like this is consistent with hands-on-keyboard enumeration but, on its own and with no corroborating process, network, or persistence evidence in the package, is insufficient to establish malicious intent. Score suspicious and investigate; do not escalate on this single indicator.
